title: [NO TESTS NEEDED] Prune and debloat
body:
* pkg/terminal: use c/storage/pkg/homedir
* turn hidden --trace into a NOP

While the homedir change is simple, the --trace changes may be more controversial.  Please speak up in case you want to keep it.  I _believe_ nobody is using it anymore and there are many parts that are not traced at all, so I think it's OK.  But I may very well miss something.

We get 2MB of binary size back.

@containers/podman-maintainers

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I'm not seeing any documentation for `--trace` anywhere in our man pages, and just wanted to double-check that you concur.
The changes LGTM and we had said we would remove this in v2.2.1.  However, at this point, I would not backport this to 3.0.  This should show up in Podman 3.1.
LGTM given the above.
